title: Complete Guide to GST Registration in India 2025
excerpt: Everything you need to know about GST registration â€” eligibility, documents required, step-by-step process, and common mistakes to avoid.
content:
## Introduction

GST (Goods and Services Tax) has transformed the Indian taxation landscape since its introduction in 2017. This comprehensive guide covers everything you need to know about GST registration in 2025.

## Who Needs to Register?

Any business with an annual turnover exceeding â‚ą40 lakhs (â‚ą20 lakhs for special category states) must register for GST. Additionally, e-commerce operators, inter-state suppliers, and certain other categories require mandatory registration regardless of turnover.

## Documents Required

1. PAN Card of the business
2. Aadhaar Card of the proprietor/partners/directors
3. Proof of business registration (if applicable)
4. Bank account details and cancelled cheque
5. Address proof of business premises
6. Digital Signature Certificate

## Step-by-Step Process

1. Visit the GST portal (www.gst.gov.in)
2. Fill Part A of Form GST REG-01
3. Receive Application Reference Number (ARN)
4. Submit required documents
5. Verification by GST officer
6. Receive GSTIN certificate

## Common Mistakes to Avoid

- Incorrect business details
- Mismatched PAN and Aadhaar details
- Incomplete document submission
- Wrong business constitution selection

## Timeline

GST registration typically takes 5-7 working days once all documents are correctly submitted.
author: AccTax Solutions Team

title: Income Tax Saving Tips for Salaried Employees FY 2025-26
excerpt: Smart tax planning strategies for salaried employees â€” 80C investments, home loan benefits, NPS, health insurance, and more.
content:
## Introduction

Tax planning is essential for maximizing your savings. Here are the best strategies for salaried employees in FY 2025-26.

## Section 80C Investments

Maximize your â‚ą1.5 lakh deduction under Section 80C through:
- PPF (Public Provident Fund)
- ELSS (Equity Linked Savings Scheme)
- NSC (National Savings Certificate)
- Life Insurance Premiums
- EPF contributions

## Home Loan Benefits

- Principal repayment: Deduction under Section 80C
- Interest payment: Deduction under Section 24(b) up to â‚ą2 lakhs

## National Pension System (NPS)

Additional deduction of â‚ą50,000 under Section 80CCD(1B)

## Health Insurance

Deduction under Section 80D for health insurance premiums
- â‚ą25,000 for self and family
- â‚ą50,000 for senior citizens

## New vs Old Tax Regime

Compare both regimes carefully before making your choice.
author: AccTax Solutions Team

title: MSME Registration: Benefits and Simplified Process
excerpt: Learn how MSME/Udyam registration can unlock subsidies, cheaper loans, and priority sector benefits for your business.
content:
## Introduction

MSME registration (now Udyam registration) is one of the most beneficial registrations for small and medium businesses in India.

## Benefits of MSME Registration

1. **Subsidized Loans**: Lower interest rates on business loans
2. **Government Subsidies**: Various subsidy schemes for registered MSMEs
3. **Tax Exemptions**: Certain tax benefits under direct and indirect taxes
4. **Priority Sector Lending**: Easier access to credit
5. **Protection Against Delayed Payments**: Interest on delayed payments from buyers

## Eligibility Criteria

- Micro Enterprise: Investment < â‚ą1 crore, Turnover < â‚ą5 crore
- Small Enterprise: Investment < â‚ą10 crore, Turnover < â‚ą50 crore
- Medium Enterprise: Investment < â‚ą50 crore, Turnover < â‚ą250 crore

## Documents Required

- Aadhaar Number
- PAN Card
- Business details
- Bank account information

## How to Register

1. Visit the Udyam Registration portal
2. Enter Aadhaar number
3. Fill business details
4. Submit and receive Udyam Registration Number

The entire process is online and free of cost.
author: AccTax Solutions Team

title: New vs Old Tax Regime: Which is Better for You?
excerpt: Detailed comparison of new and old tax regimes with calculations to help you make an informed decision for FY 2025-26.
content:
## Introduction

With the new tax regime becoming the default, it's important to understand which regime works best for you.

## Old Tax Regime

- Requires investments and deductions
- Higher tax rates but more exemptions
- Suitable for those who invest heavily

## New Tax Regime

- Lower tax rates
- No deductions or exemptions
- Simplified calculation

## Tax Slabs Comparison

**Old Regime Slabs:**
- Up to â‚ą2.5L: Nil
- â‚ą2.5L - â‚ą5L: 5%
- â‚ą5L - â‚ą10L: 20%
- Above â‚ą10L: 30%

**New Regime Slabs:**
- Up to â‚ą3L: Nil
- â‚ą3L - â‚ą7L: 5%
- â‚ą7L - â‚ą10L: 10%
- â‚ą10L - â‚ą12L: 15%
- â‚ą12L - â‚ą15L: 20%
- Above â‚ą15L: 30%

## Which One to Choose?

If you have significant investments (80C, home loan, NPS), the old regime may be better. If you prefer simplicity and have fewer deductions, choose the new regime.
author: AccTax Solutions Team

title: Understanding TDS: A Complete Guide for Businesses
excerpt: Everything about TDS â€” applicable sections, rates, due dates, return filing, and common compliance pitfalls to avoid.
content:
## Introduction

Tax Deducted at Source (TDS) is a crucial aspect of Indian tax compliance. This guide covers everything businesses need to know.

## What is TDS?

TDS is a mechanism where the payer deducts tax before making payment to the payee and deposits it with the government.

## Key TDS Sections and Rates

- Section 192: Salary (as per applicable slab)
- Section 194A: Interest (10%)
- Section 194C: Contract payments (1% for individuals/HUF, 2% for others)
- Section 194I: Rent (10% for plant/machinery, 10% for others)
- Section 194J: Professional/technical fees (10%)

## TDS Return Due Dates

- Q1 (Apr-Jun): July 31
- Q2 (Jul-Sep): October 31
- Q3 (Oct-Dec): January 31
- Q4 (Jan-Mar): May 31

## Penalties for Non-Compliance

- Late filing: â‚ą200 per day
- Late payment: 1.5% per month interest
- Non-filing: Penalty up to â‚ą1 lakh

## Common Mistakes

- Incorrect PAN of deductee
- Wrong TDS rate applied
- Late deposit of TDS
- Mismatch in Form 26AS
author: AccTax Solutions Team

title: ROC Compliance Calendar 2025: Key Deadlines
excerpt: Stay compliant with our comprehensive ROC compliance calendar covering all important filing deadlines for FY 2025-26.
content:
## Introduction

ROC (Registrar of Companies) compliance is mandatory for all registered companies in India. Here's your complete compliance calendar for 2025.

## Annual Compliance Requirements

1. **Annual Return (Form MGT-7)**: Due by November 29, 2025
2. **Financial Statements (Form AOC-4)**: Due by October 29, 2025
3. **Income Tax Return**: Due by October 31, 2025
4. **Director KYC (DIR-3 KYC)**: Due by September 30, 2025

## Quarterly Compliance

- Board Meetings: At least 4 per year (once per quarter)
- Shareholding Pattern Changes: Report within 30 days

## Event-Based Filings

- Director Changes: Form DIR-12 within 30 days
- Registered Office Change: Form INC-22 within 30 days
- Share Transfer: Form SH-4 within 60 days

## Penalties for Late Filing

- Additional fees of â‚ą100 per day per form
- Late filing of annual returns: â‚ą200 per day
- Strike off of company name for persistent non-compliance

## Best Practices

- Maintain proper board meeting minutes
- Keep statutory registers updated
- File all forms before extended deadlines
- Engage a professional for compliance management
author: AccTax Solutions Team
